review_text: Ive been using the OnePlus Nord 5 for a few weeks now, and honestly, its one of the best allround midrange phones Ive tried in a long time. The Snapdragon 8s Gen 3 delivers incredible speed multitasking and gaming (especially BGMI and COD Mobile) feel supersmooth thanks to the 144 Hz AMOLED display. The 6.83inch screen is bright and crisp, easily visible under sunlight, and the 3000 Hz touch response makes gameplay really sharp and responsive. Battery life is impressive. The massive 6,800 mAh cell easily lasts over a day and a half with moderate use, and 80 W fast charging tops it up in roughly 40 minutes. Temperature control is handled well, and even after extended gaming sessions, the phone stays comfortably warm. The camera performance is solid for the price the 50 MP main sensor delivers detailed, wellexposed shots, and the 50 MP selfie camera is among the best in this segment with sharp, lively images even in low light. The 8 MP ultrawide lens is acceptable but could use some improvement in lowlight and edge detail. Video recording at 4K 60 fps on both front and rear cameras is a pleasant surprise. Designwise, the Nord 5 feels premium with its matteglass back. Its slightly on the heavier side, but the build feels durable and premium in hand. OxygenOS is still one of the cleanest and smoothest Android skins around, with plenty of thoughtful AI features and minimal bloat ware. If I had to nitpick, the phone isnt fully waterproof (IP65 only) and the cameras, while good, dont quite match flagship levels. But for under 30,000, its hard to complain.Pros:Blazing fast Snapdragon 8s Gen 3 performanceSmooth 144 Hz AMOLED displayStrong battery life + 80 W fast chargingExcellent selfie camera and 4K videoClean, fast OxygenOS experienceCons:Slightly heavy and bulkyUltrawide camera underperforms in low lightOnly IP65 water resistanceVerdict:If you want a phone thats built for speed, gorgeous to use, and easily lasts a full day or more, the OnePlus Nord 5 is one of the smartest buys right now in the 25,00030,000 rangeRead more

review_text: Ive been using the OnePlus Nord 5 for a few weeks now, and honestly, its one of the best allround midrange phones Ive tried in a long time. The Snapdragon 8s Gen 3 delivers incredible speed multitasking and gaming (especially BGMI and COD Mobile) feel supersmooth thanks to the 144 Hz AMOLED display. The 6.83inch screen is bright and crisp, easily visible under sunlight, and the 3000 Hz touch response makes gameplay really sharp and responsive. Battery life is impressive. The massive 6,800 mAh cell easily lasts over a day and a half with moderate use, and 80 W fast charging tops it up in roughly 40 minutes. Temperature control is handled well, and even after extended gaming sessions, the phone stays comfortably warm. The camera performance is solid for the price the 50 MP main sensor delivers detailed, wellexposed shots, and the 50 MP selfie camera is among the best in this segment with sharp, lively images even in low light. The 8 MP ultrawide lens is acceptable but could use some improvement in lowlight and edge detail. Video recording at 4K 60 fps on both front and rear cameras is a pleasant surprise. Designwise, the Nord 5 feels premium with its matteglass back. Its slightly on the heavier side, but the build feels durable and premium in hand. OxygenOS is still one of the cleanest and smoothest Android skins around, with plenty of thoughtful AI features and minimal bloat ware. If I had to nitpick, the phone isnt fully waterproof (IP65 only) and the cameras, while good, dont quite match flagship levels. But for under 30,000, its hard to complain.Pros:Blazing fast Snapdragon 8s Gen 3 performanceSmooth 144 Hz AMOLED displayStrong battery life + 80 W fast chargingExcellent selfie camera and 4K videoClean, fast OxygenOS experienceCons:Slightly heavy and bulkyUltrawide camera underperforms in low lightOnly IP65 water resistanceVerdict:If you want a phone thats built for speed, gorgeous to use, and easily lasts a full day or more, the OnePlus Nord 5 is one of the smartest buys right now in the 25,00030,000 rangeRead more

review_text: Ive been using the OnePlus Nord 5 for a few weeks now, and honestly, its one of the best allround midrange phones Ive tried in a long time. The Snapdragon 8s Gen 3 delivers incredible speed multitasking and gaming (especially BGMI and COD Mobile) feel supersmooth thanks to the 144 Hz AMOLED display. The 6.83inch screen is bright and crisp, easily visible under sunlight, and the 3000 Hz touch response makes gameplay really sharp and responsive. Battery life is impressive. The massive 6,800 mAh cell easily lasts over a day and a half with moderate use, and 80 W fast charging tops it up in roughly 40 minutes. Temperature control is handled well, and even after extended gaming sessions, the phone stays comfortably warm. The camera performance is solid for the price the 50 MP main sensor delivers detailed, wellexposed shots, and the 50 MP selfie camera is among the best in this segment with sharp, lively images even in low light. The 8 MP ultrawide lens is acceptable but could use some improvement in lowlight and edge detail. Video recording at 4K 60 fps on both front and rear cameras is a pleasant surprise. Designwise, the Nord 5 feels premium with its matteglass back. Its slightly on the heavier side, but the build feels durable and premium in hand. OxygenOS is still one of the cleanest and smoothest Android skins around, with plenty of thoughtful AI features and minimal bloat ware. If I had to nitpick, the phone isnt fully waterproof (IP65 only) and the cameras, while good, dont quite match flagship levels. But for under 30,000, its hard to complain.Pros:Blazing fast Snapdragon 8s Gen 3 performanceSmooth 144 Hz AMOLED displayStrong battery life + 80 W fast chargingExcellent selfie camera and 4K videoClean, fast OxygenOS experienceCons:Slightly heavy and bulkyUltrawide camera underperforms in low lightOnly IP65 water resistanceVerdict:If you want a phone thats built for speed, gorgeous to use, and easily lasts a full day or more, the OnePlus Nord 5 is one of the smartest buys right now in the 25,00030,000 rangeRead more

review_text: Ive been using the OnePlus Nord 5 for a few weeks now, and honestly, its one of the best allround midrange phones Ive tried in a long time. The Snapdragon 8s Gen 3 delivers incredible speed multitasking and gaming (especially BGMI and COD Mobile) feel supersmooth thanks to the 144 Hz AMOLED display. The 6.83inch screen is bright and crisp, easily visible under sunlight, and the 3000 Hz touch response makes gameplay really sharp and responsive. Battery life is impressive. The massive 6,800 mAh cell easily lasts over a day and a half with moderate use, and 80 W fast charging tops it up in roughly 40 minutes. Temperature control is handled well, and even after extended gaming sessions, the phone stays comfortably warm. The camera performance is solid for the price the 50 MP main sensor delivers detailed, wellexposed shots, and the 50 MP selfie camera is among the best in this segment with sharp, lively images even in low light. The 8 MP ultrawide lens is acceptable but could use some improvement in lowlight and edge detail. Video recording at 4K 60 fps on both front and rear cameras is a pleasant surprise. Designwise, the Nord 5 feels premium with its matteglass back. Its slightly on the heavier side, but the build feels durable and premium in hand. OxygenOS is still one of the cleanest and smoothest Android skins around, with plenty of thoughtful AI features and minimal bloat ware. If I had to nitpick, the phone isnt fully waterproof (IP65 only) and the cameras, while good, dont quite match flagship levels. But for under 30,000, its hard to complain.Pros:Blazing fast Snapdragon 8s Gen 3 performanceSmooth 144 Hz AMOLED displayStrong battery life + 80 W fast chargingExcellent selfie camera and 4K videoClean, fast OxygenOS experienceCons:Slightly heavy and bulkyUltrawide camera underperforms in low lightOnly IP65 water resistanceVerdict:If you want a phone thats built for speed, gorgeous to use, and easily lasts a full day or more, the OnePlus Nord 5 is one of the smartest buys right now in the 25,00030,000 rangeRead more
